meta.title: Bench to Product Company — .NET Full-Stack Comeback
meta.note: Days 1-14 are explicit restart days. Day 15 onward is generated from phases + weekday focus + the task bank. The roadmap is the spine; AI only adapts within the current phase.

consistencyRules: Never Zero: do at least one tiny thing every day. 10 minutes counts.
Two-Day Rule: missing one day is rest. Never miss two in a row.
No-Guilt Reset: missed tasks are not debt. You do not carry yesterday into today.
A low-energy day kept the chain alive. That is a win.

phases.name: .NET Core & Web API
phases.focus: Become employable in core .NET interviews. Build the Employee API.

phases.name: SQL Depth & Project Defense
phases.focus: Build calm SQL writing. Defend every resume project.

phases.name: React Practical
phases.focus: Handle real frontend interview basics. Build an admin users page.

phases.name: Angular Practical
phases.focus: Cover the framework you claim, strongly enough.

phases.name: Azure & AI Integration
phases.focus: Make your differentiator real and explainable.

phases.name: System Design
phases.focus: Structure answers like a mid-level engineer.

phases.name: Mock Interview Sprint
phases.focus: Pressure practice. Coding, SQL, .NET, behavioral, project defense.

phases.name: Resume / Portfolio / GitHub
phases.focus: Make your story easy to trust. Align resume, portfolio, GitHub.

phases.name: Applications & Real Interviews
phases.focus: Apply with calm repetition. Every interview becomes practice data.

tasks.title: Hello World + variables + array
tasks.detail: Write Hello World, print your name, create int/string/bool/double variables, an array of 5 numbers, print it with for AND foreach.

tasks.title: What Main does in C#
tasks.detail: Read the C# program structure page. Write 5 bullet notes on Main, namespaces, and entry point.

tasks.title: Explain Main out loud
tasks.detail: Record 60 seconds: what Main is and why every C# program needs an entry point.

tasks.title: Write 4 small methods
tasks.detail: Add(int a,int b), IsEven(int n), GetMax(int[] numbers), CountPositiveNumbers(int[] numbers). Type them, do not copy.

tasks.title: Parameters & return types
tasks.detail: Note the difference between parameters, arguments, return types, and void.

tasks.title: Explain method parameters
tasks.detail: Record 60 seconds explaining parameters and return type with one example.

tasks.title: 5 array/string drills
tasks.detail: Sum array, reverse array, reverse string, count vowels, check palindrome.

tasks.title: String immutability
tasks.detail: Why strings are immutable in C#, and what string indexing means. 5 notes.

tasks.title: Explain string immutability
tasks.detail: Record 60 seconds on array indexing and why strings are immutable.

tasks.title: List + Dictionary drills
tasks.detail: Create List<string> of names, add/remove, build a word-frequency Dictionary, count each character in a string.

tasks.title: List vs Array; Dictionary uses
tasks.detail: When to use a List vs an Array, and a real Dictionary use case. 5 notes.

tasks.title: Explain List vs Array
tasks.detail: Record 60 seconds: List vs Array and one Dictionary use case.

tasks.title: Employee class
tasks.detail: Create Employee class with Id, Name, Department, Salary. Make 3 employees. Print only active ones.

tasks.title: Class vs object
tasks.detail: Difference between a class (blueprint) and an object (instance). Properties vs fields. 5 notes.

tasks.title: Explain class vs object
tasks.detail: Record 60 seconds with the blueprint analogy.

tasks.title: Inheritance + interface
tasks.detail: Person base class, Employee derived class, IWork interface, implement DoWork().

tasks.title: Inheritance vs interface
tasks.detail: When to use inheritance vs an interface. Abstract class vs interface. 5 notes.

tasks.title: Explain inheritance & interface
tasks.detail: Record 90 seconds with a real example.

tasks.title: Rewrite days 1-6 from memory
tasks.detail: No looking. Then redo the 2 exercises that need another pass.

tasks.title: Focus notes
tasks.detail: Write down the 3 things that need a calmer second pass. These become next week's focus.

tasks.title: Tell me about yourself
tasks.detail: Record TMAY (75 sec) and 'what did I work on this week'. Save both as baseline.

tasks.title: 5 SELECT queries
tasks.detail: On Employees/Departments/LeaveRequests: SELECT all, active only, ORDER BY salary, TOP 5 salaries, WHERE department = 'IT'.

tasks.title: SELECT / WHERE / ORDER BY
tasks.detail: Refresh clause order and what each does. 5 notes.

tasks.title: Explain SELECT/WHERE/ORDER BY
tasks.detail: Record 60 seconds.

tasks.title: 4 join queries
tasks.detail: Employees with department name, all employees even with no department, departments with employee count, employees who never applied for leave.

tasks.title: INNER vs LEFT JOIN
tasks.detail: Visualise the difference. When rows drop vs stay. 5 notes.

tasks.title: Explain INNER vs LEFT JOIN
tasks.detail: Record 60 seconds with an example.

tasks.title: 4 grouping queries
tasks.detail: Count employees by department, average salary by department, departments having > 5 employees, monthly leave count.

tasks.title: GROUP BY & HAVING
tasks.detail: Difference between WHERE and HAVING. 5 notes.

tasks.title: Explain GROUP BY & HAVING
tasks.detail: Record 60 seconds.

tasks.title: JS array-method drills
tasks.detail: Filter active users, map to names, sum salaries with reduce, group by department manually.

tasks.title: let / const / array methods
tasks.detail: let vs const, and map/filter/reduce. 5 notes.

tasks.title: Explain let/const & array methods
tasks.detail: Record 60 seconds.

tasks.title: Promise + async/await + fetch
tasks.detail: Create a Promise, use async/await, fetch a sample API, handle errors with try/catch.

tasks.title: Promise vs async/await
tasks.detail: What async/await is doing under the hood. 5 notes.

tasks.title: Explain Promise vs async/await
tasks.detail: Record 60 seconds.

tasks.title: Design the Employee API
tasks.detail: Write endpoints, tables, DTOs, service methods, validation rules in a notes file. No heavy coding.

tasks.title: REST design basics
tasks.detail: Resources, verbs, status codes, PUT vs PATCH. 5 notes.

tasks.title: Explain your API design
tasks.detail: Record 3 minutes walking through the design.

tasks.title: Self mock — coding
tasks.detail: Without notes: explain Main, reverse a string, write one SQL join. Time yourself.

tasks.title: Review the mock
tasks.detail: What was unclear? Where did you pause? Which topic needs a second pass? Write next week's first fix.

tasks.title: Full mock answer set
tasks.detail: Record: TMAY, explain HRMS project, 'what if you do not know an answer'. Listen once, note ONE fix.
